evals.name: draw-crawl-space
evals.prompt: Write a complete, production-quality Ruby method `draw_crawl_space_foundation` inside the `DooleyBuildingSolutions::HouseShellBuilder` module for SketchUp. The method should accept: `entities` (SketchUp Entities), `perimeter_pts` (array of [x,y] pairs in inches defining the building footprint), `stem_height_in` (Float, height of the crawl space stem wall in inches), `vent_cfg` (Hash with keys :width_in, :height_in, :spacing_in). It should generate: (1) perimeter stem wall segments using draw_box, with vent openings cut using interval subtraction, (2) correct material using the material cache pattern, (3) BIM data attribution with IfcFooting and Concrete. Follow all coding standards: operation wrapping, namespacing, no eval, specific exception handling, method under 20 lines where possible.
evals.expected_output: Proper DooleyBuildingSolutions::HouseShellBuilder module namespace. model.start_operation / model.commit_operation with rescue/abort. draw_box calls for wall segments. subtract_intervals for vent positions. @materials_cache pattern. apply_bim_data call with 'Footing'. No bare rescue. ft_to_in conversion at boundary. Methods under 20 lines.
